title: Keep effects as values, run them at the edge
description: Describe side effects as IO/effect values and compose them, deferring execution to a single run at the program boundary.
body: Use when writing side-effecting code with Cats-Effect/ZIO (or designing pure cores). Do not perform side effects inline in the middle of a computation or start work the moment you construct it. Actions: wrap effects in IO/Task so they are descriptions, compose them with map/flatMap/for, and run the resulting program once (unsafeRunSync / IOApp) at the outermost edge. Validation signals include a test that constructing the effect performs nothing until it is run, preserving referential transparency. Risk boundary: an eagerly executed effect breaks composition and makes retries/cancellation impossible; mixing eager side effects into a supposedly pure value is a defect that undermines the whole effect system.
